product: Wireless Bluetooth headphones with noise cancellation

prediction.Review:
Alright, so I just got these wireless Bluetooth headphones, and I'm really digging them! First off, the setup was incredibly simple. My phone picked them up in seconds, and the Bluetooth connection has been consistently stable – no annoying dropouts, which is a major win for me. The sound quality? Absolutely excellent. The audio is super crisp, and the bass has a nice, deep punch without being muddy. Podcasts sound clear, and music just comes alive. Plus, the battery life is seriously impressive; I can go for days of regular use (commutes, work calls, general listening) before even thinking about charging them. That's a huge convenience.

Now, on to the noise cancellation. It does a *decent* job, I'll say that. It definitely knocks out a lot of the low-frequency drone on the bus or in a noisy office, making things much calmer. However, it's not a complete 'bubble' effect. You'll still pick up on clearer voices or sudden, louder sounds, so don't expect total silence. They feel quite comfortable for shorter periods, but after a couple of hours, I do start to feel a bit of pressure on my ears. Not a deal-breaker, but worth noting if you plan to wear them for an entire workday straight. The controls are well-placed and intuitive, which I appreciate.

Overall, I think these headphones are a fantastic buy. The sound is top-notch, the wireless connection is flawless, and the battery goes on and on. While the noise cancellation is more 'good' than 'great,' and comfort has a slight limit for marathon sessions, they offer a really strong package. Definitely a solid recommendation for anyone wanting a quality wireless listening experience without breaking the bank!

history.reviewText: The rubber seems "molded", meaning it doesn't stretch as easily as those rubber bands. I'm not sure how this can help with drops or bumps, but it's good enough for everyday mishaps. This covers the whole phone, including volume buttons and power key. All other buttons are carefully accessible, so nothing should get "stuck" by the cover, even that IR sensor.I personally prefer this type of case, which puts a layer, to further protect the camera lens, when placed on a flat surface. The covers that replace the phone's battery cover will not protect the camera lens, in my opinion. I just don't think the camera lens is Gorilla Corning glass, that's all.You should be aware that even though it says White, the edges are gray.

I travel about 150K mi/year for work, and I used to bring a Bose unit to listen to music in my hotel room.  The 'Mini is about the same size as the Bose's power supply, but still produces really nice sound.  Not just for its size, but in general.  It doesn't have the "kick" of the Bose, but if you tweak up the bass on your EQ settings then it's a pretty close second.And since it charges with the same micro-USB cables I carry already, that's even more stuff I can toss overboard.Jawbone got this one right.

I originally got this so i could strap my phone on my wrist to leave my hands free to walk and play with my kids, I could also carry ID if we ventured too far. Although great for what I got it for, it is a bit difficult to talk on the phone if you need to since you have to open the entire wallet to answer the phone and expose all of your personal information (credit cards and IDs) sitting on the opposite  side. Its a bit tricky...

Very lightweight bluetooth headset. I currently use one now at the moment and without a ear hook it fits inside the ear snug and tight. There are never any worries about it falling out or becoming misplaced. After 30 minutes of use i barely noticed it in my ear. Perfect for someone who hates bulk and the mess of using an ear hook to keep it in. The voice quality is very high compared to other higher priced bluetooth headsets. Made lightweight for the casual user

I think the looks and comfort of this headset are phenomenal. This is a comfortable headset that doesn't scream for attention and allows me to have a functional hands-free phone experience. I will get right down to the pros and cons:PROS:* Comfortable to wear and stay in place.* Very good call reception quality.* Decent stereo music playback quality.* Fairly good battery life.* Universal USB charging port.* Easy pairing.* Good looks that don't look ridiculous or "rude".CONS:* Sometimes very poor call quality when talking. I've had people complain that they had a hard time understanding me while I'm driving (windows shut, stereo off). I think it amplifies ambient sound that will drown out voice.* Music sound quality requires a bass boost. I've found that this headset is geared more for voices in the middle and higher ranges. Music can sound tinny.* There is a need for an adjustable headband. The headset stays on, but can sometimes feel like it's flopping in the back. I have a medium (7 1/4) hat size.I don't think this product is quite "there", especially if you are looking to use it for phone calls. I use mine primarily with a BlackBerry Pearl and a Motorola Q and the better sound quality comes from the BlackBerry, so the type of device you are using with it does matter.Still, I received this as a gift for Christmas, 2007, and was excited at the time. I would suggest looking in to the newer version, the Motorola S9-HD, which has improved sound quality:[...]

product: Wireless Bluetooth headphones with noise cancellation

prediction.Review:
Finally got around to trying these wireless Bluetooth headphones with noise cancellation, and honestly, the experience is a mixed bag. The core idea is great, but the execution has some glaring flaws that really detract from daily use.

First, the good points: The sound quality is decent enough for everyday listening. It's not going to blow anyone away, but music and podcasts are clear, though the bass can feel a bit muddy. The Bluetooth pairing process is straightforward and quick, which is a plus. The noise cancellation *does* work, to an extent. It effectively dampens constant background hums, like an air conditioner or the rumble of a bus, making those environments a bit more peaceful. It's not going to make voices disappear, but it helps create a quieter personal space.

Now, for the issues that prevent these from being truly good. My biggest gripe is comfort. These headphones have a clamping force that is just too tight. After about 30 minutes, my ears start to ache, and the earcups get uncomfortably warm. It feels like they weren't designed for anyone to wear them for extended periods, which completely defeats the purpose of wireless headphones. I'm quite sensitive to how things feel, and this is a major problem. They just don't feel good on the head.

The build quality is another letdown. There's a lot of creaky plastic, making them feel rather flimsy. They don't inspire confidence in their durability, and I have concerns about how long the thin earpads will last before showing wear. I wish more attention had been paid to the materials.

The controls are also poorly thought out. All the buttons are lined up on one earcup and feel almost identical to the touch. Trying to adjust the volume, skip a track, or activate ANC blindly is a guessing game, leading to constant fumbling. There's a definite lack of tactile feedback, which is a significant design oversight.

Overall, these headphones are just passable. They provide basic wireless audio and some noise cancellation, but the persistent comfort issues and frustrating controls make them difficult to recommend. If you're sensitive to comfort and expect intuitive controls, you'll likely be disappointed. They get the job done, but with too many compromises.

history.reviewText: I've been putting screen protectors on since I had a Palm Pilot 10 years ago. Me and the wife have both had the iPhone 3G, 4, and now 5, plus two iPads. All of them got screen protectors. I've never had a more difficult time getting a screen protector on dust free. I'm pretty sure these came with dust on them, both of them. I kept them facing down, directly over the screen of the phone before I removed the first film. Wiped the screen, and laid these down. Several large specs of dust and bubbles. Pulled up, removed the dust with tape, back down, more dust. It's like it attracts dust out of the air. I've never had a screen protector do this. Another thing, they aren't cut right. You can either line them up with the camera and speaker up top, or the home button. Not both. Threw both of my 2-pack in the trash. That's what I get for going cheap. Back to Zagg, maybe overpriced but at least they go on dust free and hold up.

HTC One is the best phone right now.  Period.Nexus 4? No LTE.Galaxy S4? Cheap-feeling case, lacks simplicity - too many features crammed in.iPhone 5?  This is replacing my iPhone - a phone I thought to be near perfect. It was solid, attractive, fast, and had a beautiful screen.  But the HTC One is all that and more, it's running a mature Android 4 OS, it has a great camera, it's crazy fast, and the screen is incredible without being oversized.  Not to mention all the features I never knew I wanted, like front-facing stereo sound.  I can show my friends a video in a crowded bar, and we can all hear it.And it's wonderful to hold.  Once you get this piece of metal in your hands, every other phone is going to feel like cheap last-gen technology.  Including the iPhone 5.If you're interested in phone hacking, there's good news: Google is re-branding the HTC One as its own and will sell an unlocked version.  This should mean a healthy & long development life for this phone.Highly, highly recommended.

If you are planning to attach this Bolse Mini Car Universal Holder to your windshield, don't consider buying it if you live in a cold climate.  I'm simply calling this holder a fair weather friend.  I wanted to like it, but it's simply not a practicable tool for colder climates.  Clean windshield or no, it simply won't stick to mine for very long without clattering down.  I've tried several Bolse products and have been quite satisfied with them, but this one is not a "universal product."However, if you are in a warmer climate, this mini holder may suit your needs.  It definitely is solid and I do like the way it rotates.  The inside of the clamp is well-padded and when closed will readily hold your iPhone, GPS (maximum width of 2 3/4"), Galaxy, or Mp3.  There are several nice things about it, but cannot recommend it to anyone living in any winter wonderland.Product Features:~  Material:  ABS & PVC~  Color:  Black~  Holding Range:  5.5cm ~ 8.5cm~  360 degree rotation in all directions~  You can put your cell phone (iPhone 5, Galaxy) in several positions.~  Quick installationSample provided by distributor.

This is the best screen protector I have ever used. I am a gadget freak, I buy and sell phones and tablets almost weekly and every device I get my hands on ends up with an iSmooth screen protector. They are extremely easy to go on and are very tough, I sometimes end up with my phone and keys in the same pocket and I can honestly say I've never ended up with a  scratch on my screen protector.The other thing very annoying about screen protectors is air bubbles once you put them on, never had one with my iSmooth protectors. Also some protectors feel like crap to the touch, like sand. The feel of these protectors don't bug me.A product isn't just about the product you purchase but also the company you purchase it from and the support you get. I've dealt with items before that say they have a lifetime guarantee but there is always stipulations. I recently went through the warranty process with iSmooth on my Moto X. All I had to do was give them my Amazon order number and no questions asked I got a replacement. I will stick with iSmooth.

I needed a 5 Volt DC power supply that could be powered from a 12 Volt battery or other 12 V DC power source. This fit the bill nicely and inexpensively. Clipped the connector off and used my own connector and off to the races it went. My DVM measured 4.99 V loaded at 600 mA and 5.01 Volts unloaded so I am pleased as punch. Plus the temperature rise is only a few degrees indicating good efficiency. Interesting packaging with the Motorola logo lit with a blue led. Might mention the two leads inside the insulated wire (one red, other black) are stranded wire of about 20 guage so I can see how flexing the cable at the connector when attached to a device in an auto might cause an intermittent break. A little duct tape or heat shrink tubing at the connector might not look pretty but should keep the wire from stressing.

product: Wireless Bluetooth headphones with noise cancellation

prediction.Review:
I've been looking for a good pair of noise-cancelling headphones for a while, and these definitely deliver on most fronts. Straight out of the box, they feel sturdy and well-made, not cheap or flimsy. The Bluetooth pairing was quick and painless with my phone, and I didn't experience any dropouts or connectivity issues, even when moving around the house. The range was also pretty solid. 

The sound quality is quite good for wireless headphones. The bass is present without being overwhelming, and the mids and highs are clear enough for most music genres. Where these really shine, though, is the noise cancellation. It does an excellent job of muting constant background hums – think airplane engines, train noise, or the general office chatter. It's not absolute silence, but it significantly reduces the noise floor, making it much easier to focus on music or podcasts. Comfort is also decent; I could wear them for a couple of hours without much discomfort, though they do get a bit warm after extended use. Battery life seems solid, I haven't timed it precisely but it lasts through a full workday.

Now, on to where they could improve. While the noise cancellation is impressive for ambient sounds, it doesn't do as well with sharper, sudden noises or distinct voices. You'll still hear people talking, just muffled. Also, the controls on the earcup are a bit small and fiddly, taking some getting used to. I often found myself pressing the wrong button trying to adjust volume or skip tracks. The overall design is a bit bulky, which is common for over-ear headphones, but it's something to consider for portability, even with the included soft case. It would have been nice to have a more compact folding mechanism for easier travel.

Overall, these are a very capable pair of wireless noise-cancelling headphones. They offer great sound and effective noise cancellation where it matters most, making them a good option for travel or noisy environments. The minor issues with specific noise cancellation types and the fiddly controls keep it from being perfect, but they're still a solid choice.

history.reviewText: I bought this product to pair with my BlackBerry Z10. I can report the two devices paired up without any problems. The connection between the two is very stable. So if you can find the headset at a reasonable price and you want to pair it to a BlackBerry you can hardly go wrong.ProsLong battery life.Good sound quality.Good voice pickup.Simple setup and configuration.ConsTakes a lot of time to get a perfect fit with the ear.A bit nose heavy.Looks like it was designed for a BlackBerry Bold.Voice menu is limited and not all that accurate.

My husband and I each own Galaxy Note 2 phones and we love these cases!  He can still fit the phone (with the case on) in his pants pocket for work.  The screen protectors are a bit tricky to install, but be patient and make sure the screen is clean before you begin.Hubby also uses this padded case http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B009RI0C9A/ref=oh_details_o05_s00_i01?ie=UTF8&psc;=1  for extra protection when he puts his phone in his backpack or when he's traveling.  The phone and Otterbox case slip in and out easily and there is a super velcro closure to keep it secure.  He's only used the clip so far - not the belt loop attachment.

I've ordered home buttons before and tho I knew this was an order for 1 only, I think they could have sent a sheet of 4-6 for the same price. But that's my opinion. As far as functionality, it works well and was easy to apply and sticks well. You would not be able to reapply if you don't get it on the home button right the first time, so apply carefully. Appearance is just what you expect...frivolous bling for your phone!  They are raised gems and look great in combination with a bling bling case. :)

This mount is a very useful accessory for anyone using a smartphone as gps or music source in the car.  Sturdy mount & holds your device securely.  Putting your device in line of site certainly increases usefulness & your safety.  Take 30 seconds to read the FAQ and then decide where to mount.  This mount has the right fixtures, is well made and like other AmazonBasics devices I've bought is a great value.11/18 - Just figured out the mount is compatible with a garmin weighted GPS mount.  just detach the part that actually holds your phone & it appears to be compatible with ball mounts from other devices (at least this garmin bean bag mount.)  That's a plus for me.

I have had a few bluetooths in my time and i must say that this is my favorite thus far. The battery lasts me 10 hours on avareage! that is unheard of for most bluetooths! The sound is crisp and clear with just enough bass. Great way to just cut out the rest of the world and just do your thing. I use it for work because i am moving around a lot and for working out. In both respects it is amazing! It has great range It will cut out when you face away from your phone but only when you are like 30 feet away and what bluetooth doesnt. I love that it vibrates when you are getting a call as well. I have never had anyone complain about me when i have been using it either so that is good too. all around a good buy and would recommend to anyone.

product: Wireless Bluetooth headphones with noise cancellation

prediction.Review:
Wow, these headphones are absolutely fantastic! I've been looking for a solid pair for ages, and these wireless Bluetooth beauties with noise cancellation totally deliver. They've really changed my daily commute – no more dreadful city noise, just pure audio bliss!

**Noise Cancellation:** This is the star of the show, hands down! It's simply phenomenal. Turn it on, and the world just *melts away*. Whether I'm on a packed train or trying to focus in a bustling coffee shop, it creates an incredible bubble of quiet. There's a slight, almost imperceptible pressure when it's engaged, but honestly, it's a small price to pay for such incredible peace.

**Sound Quality:** Punchy and vibrant! The audio is rich and clear, with a fantastic bass presence that isn't overwhelming or muddy. Music sounds alive, and even podcasts get a boost. Phone calls are crystal clear on both ends too – definitely a win!

**Comfort:** I can wear these for hours without any discomfort. The earcups are super soft and breathable, and the headband has just the right amount of padding. They feel secure but not tight, which is perfect for long listening sessions or even light activity.

**Bluetooth & Battery:** Pairing was an absolute breeze with my phone and laptop – literally seconds! The connection is rock-solid, no annoying dropouts when I walk between rooms. Range is pretty good, definitely covers my apartment, though maybe not the *super-human* distance they advertise, but who needs to walk that far from their device anyway? And the battery? Seriously impressive! I get days of heavy use, even with NC constantly on.

**Controls:** Okay, so if I had one tiny nitpick, it's the controls. They're a bit on the small side, and sometimes I accidentally hit the volume instead of the NC toggle. It takes a little muscle memory, but once you're used to it, it's manageable. Not a deal-breaker, just something to note.

**Overall:** These headphones are a total powerhouse and an absolute keeper! The incredible noise cancellation, amazing sound, and fantastic comfort far outweigh any minor quibbles with the controls. If you're craving peace and killer audio, snatch these up! Highly, highly recommended!

history.reviewText: I am not going into how great this phone is - nice, large screen, fast, great colors etc.,If you are planning to sign up with ATT GoPhone, this might help:I went with ATT GoPhone for $25 a month plus $5 data. I got this activated on GoPhone but my data connection doesnt work!. I was not ready to believe that this is a faulty phone and went to the local ATT store. Here is what they had to do to fix the phone to start working with ATT data. This may not be obvious as the store person had to ingeniously work this out from another ATT branded phone after the Tech Help was unwilling to help saying that they dont support non-ATT phones. (ATT Phone = Phone that has "ATT" branded on it).If you are like me where the phone works for making calls over ATT network but data doenst work, follow these steps:Settings > Wireless and network > Mobile networks > Access Point Names. You should have two entries here:1. AT&T; PTA2. ATT WAPIf you dont see one or both of these entries, add them following these steps:STEP 1:********Menu > New APNName: AT&T; PTAAPN: ptaMMSC: [...]MMS proxy: proxy.mobile.att.netMMS port: 80MCC: 310MNC: 410APN type: internet + mms.Menu > Save.STEP 2:********Menu > New APNName: ATT WAPAPN: wap.cingularProxy: wireless.cingular.comPort: 80MMSC: [...]MMS proxy: wireless.cingular.comMMS Port: 80MCC: 310MNC: 410APN type: internet + mmsMenu > Save.STEP 3:********Go to previous screen, Select "ATT WAP". Previous screen, uncheck "Use Packet data" and turn it on again, your 3G should catch up.

BEFORE YOU READ BELOW PLEASE READ THIS: Make sure you set aside time to play with this. Your girlfriend or wife is going to hate you for it if you don't. ......haha..trust me I learned from experience.I was a blackberry user for years, and I never wanted to switch. But I wanted to see what 4G was like. Sprint is testing 4G in many more markets than are on their website (Many places in the San Francisco area and Los Angeles). Here is a break down of the pros and cons1. Once you get use to "Swype" typing, you'll never use the keyboard. I bought this phone becaue of the physical keyboard (since I came from a blackberry) but I never use it. "Swype" is great. Go look up a video.2. Battery life is pretty good despite what people say. Since the Amoled screen does not use power with black pixels. I made my wallpaper solid black. Improved battery life drastically. Trust me on this! .....Now if you use 4G it drains your battery quickly. See below. Also charging time takes a long time. It takes maybe twice as long to fully charge this phone than a blackberry and the MaH is only slightly stronger (1500). Use the "Power Widget" to very easily turn on/off things like 4G, Wifi, Adjut brightness. This will help greatly.3. 4G..It drains your battery, and mean quickly. God's honest truth......3G is fast enough. I mean 4G was cool to test out, but it really doesn't make a difference when browsing. Maybe downloading music or video (yes) but how often do you do that. When it comes to apps and browsing 3G is fast enough. I teathred my phone to my laptop, and the 4G speeds were barely faster than 3G. 3G speeds were about 1MB per second download. 4G was 1.2.  Now if you run a network speedtest on the phone at [...], it's fast...4-5 MbPS.3. Customization - Awesome, nothing like it. I love it. No complaints. You'll love it.4. Feel of the phone - Overall a little too thick for me. Once you add it the protective case, that thing aint goin in your pocket. It makes it to thick. So if you like a thin phone, this one is not for you. As I mentioned above, with the "Swype" technology you'll never find yourself using a keyboard. The keyboard also "wiggles" around a bit.....5. Charging Port - Kinda funny that's its on top of the phone, but not a big deal.6. Bluetooth - Great Quality (3.0 BT technology). Good reception from a number of Bluetooth pieces I've tried. It's also cool streaming podcasts /music to your bluetooh. Cool feature.7. Apps - App market is great. Again customizing this phone is one of the greatest features. Apps will randomly start for some reason. I don't know if they suck up battery life or cpu usage. The sprint guy told me they don't.8. Unlocking the phone - PHone has an auto lock. You have to touch the power button and then slide the screen to unlock. Get an app on the market for this called "Screen Mode" to customize this. Now I can lock / unlock my phone by just hitting a button.9. Youtube videos are great to watch on here you can watch them in HQ. Sometimes when you do, you'll have to reset your phone for it to work. I know it sounds funny, but this is a minor flaw. Youtube in HQ is great!10. E-mail and Web Browsing. Seamlessly hooked up to my company's exchange. So much more superior than using a blackberry in that regard. The way you can view the e-mails and attachments are great. Also synced with Yahoo and Gmail. Again. Awesome. No complaints.11. Web Browsing - NO other phone like it! plain and simple it's great.12. The google search feature. I know this is available on other droid phones. But the voice activated or type features are awesome!13. Phone reception / quality - Perfect.Overall. If you can get over the thickness you'll love it.

I have had great success with Anker products in the past, and this item follows suit.The 2200mAh battery SEEMS to last slightly longer than the 2100mAh stock GS3 battery. That could just be a placebo. I mainly purchased this to be able to have an extra charged battery to carry with me so I can swap it out when I get low.My GS3 sees moderate use, and is near dead after about 10-12 hours. Now I can swap out the battery for a fully charged one without worrying about it.Also I like how the charger has a USB charging port on the side. Though the voltage running off that port must be a bit low, because it has a hard time charging my android tablet, even overnight. It will fully charge my phone though, no problem.Overall pleased with the product and price.The only con is how super bright the LED charging indicator light is. Seriously it's too bright to have right next to my bed, I had to put duct tape over it.

I haven't tried any other car Bluetooth devices so I have nothing to compare this to, but I'm perfectly happy with this one.  The audio isn't CD quality by any means but it's good enough, and I've never had any pairing issues.The device is smaller than it looks online, so I was able to find a place for it to the left of the stereo console on my crowded Camry dash.  I've had it on for months and it is still sticking strong.Of course with any Bluetooth kit the performance depends greatly on the device you're pairing it with.  The Belkin worked better with my old iPhone 4S than it does with my Samsung Galaxy S3, but that's a problem with Android 4.1.1 and the software I'm trying to use with it, not the Belkin.  Still, it works well enough with my S3 that I can listen to Slacker Radio and use voice dialing to make calls while on the move, and that's all that matters.I'd recommend this to anyone looking for hands free cell capabilities in the car.

Ordered this case because of the card-holding feature and it has been great so far in that it holds exactly what I need (although I tried out the claim that it also holds folded bills, it didn't do much for me there seeing as it's a tight fit with even one card). It feels great that I can grab my phone with a few important cards in my case and my keys and be done with it - it's been very practical for school or when I don't want to carry around a bag. I was worried the color of the red case would be off from how it appears online but I was pleasantly surprised to find it's not the muted red I expected (it's not the brightest thing either - I found the color just right.)Overall I am very satisfied. The one thing I will say that this case is lacking is more of a grip to it. I also have a Candyshell Grip case for my phone and I love how secure my phone feels in my hands with that case on it - I don't get that so much with this case but that's something I knew before purchasing this case. The case also isn't as snug to the phone as I woud like it to be, but it's not too loose to the point where I'm scared my phone will pop up and the sturdy material it's made up of makes makes up for the fact that it's a tiny bit loose.

product: Wireless Bluetooth headphones with noise cancellation

prediction.Review: Oh wow, these headphones are AMAZING! Seriously, I've been looking for a great pair of wireless headphones, and these absolutely deliver. The sound quality is fantastic – super clear highs and deep, rich bass that really makes my music pop. And the noise cancellation? Game changer! I wore them on my commute today, and it was like the world just melted away. No more annoying bus chatter or street noise, just my tunes. They're super comfortable too, which is a big deal for me since I wear headphones for hours. They fit snugly without feeling too tight, and the earcups are really soft. Pairing was a breeze, connected to my phone in seconds, no fuss at all. Battery life seems solid, haven't had them die on me yet! Honestly, if you're thinking about getting some new headphones, grab these. You won't regret it! They totally exceeded my expectations. Five stars, no question!

history.reviewText: The product does what it's supposed to do, I love it's low profile and how secure it feels when it's in the cig lighter port. The only thing that I wonder about is the LED...when my Samsung GS3 is charging, the LED constantly cycles through all the colors....when it's fully charged, it still does the same. Just wondering why that is? But other than that, it charges pretty fast and is well built...no more accidentally hitting or bumping the cig lighter usb charger anymore since this thing is pretty much flush.

This is an inexpensive and essential accessory. Once you have applied the screen protector and worked out the bubbles, you can't even tell it's there. The screen protector has removable handling tabs to help minimize touching it and leaving fingerprints. Be sure to apply it in a clean environment with minimal dust. I did it in the kitchen after running a little steamy hot water to minimize dust. This works well if you don't overdo the hot water and fog the screen. The only thing I had difficulty with was getting it precisely positioned.

Bought these and was VERY skeptical even with the amount of rave reviews.I use them with my iPad and iPhone 4S and the only thing that I have found to be faulty is if you are a big EDM fan or listen to stuff with heavy bass you might not like these because they don't really hold up and it sounds distorted, it's not as bad if you turn it down a little.The ear buds fit weird, and have fallen out a few times but I wore them at work for 12 hours listening to pandora (and still have battery...had to charge my phone twice) and I was looking under things and moving around a lot, walking around I don't seem to really have that problem. I am looking into getting new rubber end pieces because I don't really like how they fit and I think it would help to keep them in more if they weren't that bubble shape.The horse collar is not obstructive and I often find that I forget it is there, and when I do I'm like "what the... oh yeah forgot about that".People complain about range but I have not had that problem considering the range for an unobstructed bluetooth signal is 30ft, now add the obstructions that we all have in our daily life (walls, furniture, floors, other wireless signals) it can cut the range down and the range I get with this is no different than the other bluetooth devices that I own now.So before blaming the device think a little about your own personal amount of interference...just some advice for all bluetooth devices :-)

My schedule is hectic. Almost every day I attend college from 8AM to 3:30PM, and right after I work from 4PM to 12PM. Both school and work involves me using my phone. Whether it's contacting clients or researching information for classroom discussions, let's just say my Galaxy S4 is always under heavy usage. Even though the S4 has a much improved battery life than the previous models, it is still no where near perfect to run an entire day on one charge. I was lucky enough to be contacted by RAVPower to try this product. When I realized this product would enable me to charge my device and a spare batery, I was very excited because this would solve my battery issues for long days. Due to my previous positive experiences with their products, I decided to try this one out without any hesitation.Within two days the product was at my doorstep. The charging dock came packaged very nicely in a box. Once I took the product out of the box, I was very impressed by the overall build and quality. For the price point, the design and attention to detail really caught my attention. The front part of the docking system has a beautiful glossy finish to match your S4's body, whereas the sides of the product has a matte finish, complementing the S4's brushed steel look. The back of the charging system has a little insert to charge a spare battery. Now, it has a little LED light to show if the battery is charging. When the LED light is glowing red, it indicates that the battery is charging. Once the charging is complete, the LED light switches from RED to GREEN. The charging time for the docking system is extremely fast, within about an hour I can charge my phone completely. The charging time for the spare battery isn't as fast, it takes about 1 hour and 40ish minutes to charge the battery completely, which isn't bad at all.For the price point, RAVpower is enabling customers of Amazon to get a gorgeous charging dock for a very low price. The quality and performance of the product does not make sense for the price point. If you are someone such as me with a busy schedule or if you are just someone who likes to put their phone under heavy usage, this is a great product for you because it will always enable you to have a spare fully charged battery, no matter where you are.RAVPower has an amazing customer service team. I've never had any problems with their products but they have always taken their time to respond to any questions I have ever had in order to make sure that I was fully content. I highly recommend this product, not only for it's performance and quality but also for the company that is behind it!

Typically, I prefer the fatter ballpoint pens with the wide tip. They just glide more smoothly than the sharp tip of a fine tip pen. That's the only reason why (for me) this is a 4 star pen/stylus combo (it has a very fine ballpoint pen tip). I also agree with one of the other reviewers, that I would have preferred it to have a knit tip on it, that's just my personal preference. However, overall this is an awesome stylus/ballpoint pen combo, and both the pen and stylus work well for their intended uses.This product was provided to me as a sample in exchange for a fair and honest review.UPDATE! I did my video review and used both the white and black pens (originally I only tried the black pen, figured they were both the same). Not so! Whether intentional or not, the white pen has a thicker, richer line than the black one. Cool!! I love the white pen, it is perfect for me! The stylus is amazing as always, but I had to put more info here because I just love how easily the white pen glides along the surface of the paper. There is nothing like writing with a pen that makes life easier. I know that probably sounds crazy, but I write A LOT and I hate having to fight with pens to get some ink to come out. This has easily become my favorite go to stylus/pen combo. Seeing how beautifully the white pen writes, I have to think the black pen with the fine point might just be a quality control issue, or maybe it's just my preference. 4.5 stars (rounds up to 5)!! :) This company never disappoints me.
